sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_epoch INT, start_time TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, start_epoch, start_time) VALUES (1, 1234567890, sys.epoch(1234567890));
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, start_epoch, start_time) VALUES (2, -1, sys.epoch(-1));
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, start_epoch, start_time) VALUES (3, NULL, NULL);
CREATE TABLE test_inets (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET, valid BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO test_inets (id, ip_address, valid) VALUES (1, inet '192.168.1.5', TRUE);
INSERT INTO test_inets (id, ip_address, valid) VALUES (2, inet '192.168.1.6', FALSE);
SELECT id, sys.epoch(start_epoch) AS timestamp FROM test_dates WHERE start_epoch < 0;
SELECT t1.id FROM test_dates t1 JOIN test_inets t2 ON t1.start_epoch < 1000000000 WHERE t2.valid;
SELECT AVG(start_epoch) FROM test_dates WHERE start_epoch IS NOT NULL GROUP BY id HAVING sys.epoch(AVG(start_epoch)) < sys.epoch(99999999);
SELECT t1.id FROM test_dates AS t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_inets AS t2 WHERE t1.start_epoch < 50 AND t2.ip_address < inet '192.168.1.7');
SELECT id, sys.epoch(COALESCE(start_epoch, 0)) FROM test_dates WHERE sys.epoch(start_epoch) < sys.epoch(1234567891);
CREATE TABLE temp_test (id INT, ip INET);
INSERT INTO temp_test (id, ip) VALUES (1, inet '192.168.0.1');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE start_epoch < (SELECT MAX(start_epoch) FROM temp_test);
SELECT t1.id FROM test_dates t1 JOIN (SELECT id FROM test_inets WHERE valid = FALSE) t2 ON t1.start_epoch < 0 AND t1.id = t2.id;
CREATE TABLE boundary_test (id INT, value TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO boundary_test (id, value) VALUES (1, sys.epoch(0));
INSERT INTO boundary_test (id, value) VALUES (2, sys.epoch(1577836800));
SELECT id, CASE WHEN value < sys.epoch(0) THEN 'past' ELSE 'future' END AS time_status FROM boundary_test;
